title: Vector network binary format decoder, encoder, and rendering utilities
purpose: Provides functions to decode binary vector network data, encode network data back to binary format, and convert vector networks into CanvasKit paths for rendering.
module_context: Exports functions to handle vector network binary data including decoding, encoding, path conversion, and bounding box computation. Defines interfaces for vector network components and style overrides.

steps.content: Read the header to determine the number of vertices, segments, and regions.
Build a mapping from style indices to override entries if a style override table is provided.
Parse each vertex entry and construct a vector vertex with position and handle mirroring.
Parse each segment entry and construct a vector segment with start/end indices and tangent vectors.
Parse each region entry including winding rule and loop structure to build vector regions.
Return the fully constructed vector network object containing vertices, segments, and regions.
Calculate the total byte size required for the binary output.
Initialize a buffer with the calculated size and a data view for writing.
Write the header containing counts of vertices, segments, and regions.
Write each vertex entry with position data and placeholder style override index.
Write each segment entry with start/end indices and tangent vectors.
Write each region entry with winding rule and loop structure.
Return the completed binary buffer as a Uint8Array.
Initialize a new CanvasKit path object for building the geometry.
IF regions are present in the network, process each region's loops to build closed paths.
ELSE process segments as open paths by building chains from connected segments.
For each chain, move to the starting point and add segments to the path.
Handle any remaining disconnected segments by adding them as individual paths.
Return the constructed path object.

methods.name: addLoopToPath
methods.normalized_params: (path: Path, loop: number[], segments: VectorSegment[], vertices: VectorVertex[]): void

steps.content: Move to the starting point of the loop.
Add each segment in the loop to the path.
IF the loop is closed, close the path.

methods.name: addSegmentToPath
methods.normalized_params: (path: Path, seg: VectorSegment, vertices: VectorVertex[]): void

steps.content: Determine if the segment is a straight line based on tangent vectors.
IF the segment is a line, add a line to the end point.
ELSE add a cubic bezier curve using the start point, tangent vectors, and end point.
Build an adjacency map of vertices to connecting segments.
Initialize tracking for visited segments and result chains.
Identify degree-1 vertices as starting points for chain building.
For each starting vertex, traverse connected segments to form chains.
Return the list of segment chains.

methods.name: computeVectorBounds
methods.normalized_params: (network: VectorNetwork): { x: number; y: number; width: number; height: number }

steps.content: IF no vertices exist, return default zero bounds.
Initialize bounds with extreme values for min/max coordinates.
Update bounds based on vertex positions.
Update bounds to include control points of bezier curves.
Calculate and return the final bounding box dimensions.
